fresh_facts.claim: The US-Iran war began on February 28, 2026, and remains technically in a ceasefire as of April 24, 2026, with the conflict nearing 60 days duration.
The Pentagon authorized the US Navy to 'shoot and kill' Iranian fast boats laying mines or disrupting passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
US forces recently seized another tanker smuggling Iranian oil, expanding the naval blockade on Iranian ports and causing over 10% GDP loss (~$5 billion).
Trump claimed US strikes have 'knocked out' 70-90% of Iran's missile and drone production and defense industrial base.
US intelligence assessments note that Iran retains thousands of operational missiles and drones hidden underground despite US strikes.
The IRGC seized Israel-linked commercial vessels on April 22, 2026, with first video evidence since the war began, while enforcing a Hormuz blockade.
Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the Supreme Leader, praised Iran's 'extraordinary unity' and warned of psychological warfare hours after vessel seizures.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on April 21, 2026, for discussions including potential US peace talks.
Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, head of Iran's US negotiating team, quit amid internal disagreements over diplomacy.
IRGC Commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi opposes US peace talks and acts as a key conduit to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, creating internal divisions.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli media reported on April 19 that inability to contact Supreme Leader Khamenei hinders US-Iran peace talks.
The Iran-US ceasefire was set to expire on April 22, 2026, with Islamabad talks positioned as a potential 'final diplomatic off-ramp' before escalation.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Trump warned that military action will resume if US-Iran diplomacy fails.
The US Treasury froze $344 million in cryptocurrency tied to Iran.
The US imposed sanctions on a Chinese refinery and dozens of vessels for involvement in Iran oil trade.
The Institute for the Study of War tracks ongoing protests as the regime's most serious unrest ever, with brutal crackdowns including internet blackouts.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Between 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi fighters entered Iran in early 2026 to assist with suppressing protests, per CNN and Fox News sources.
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged legislation to proscribe the IRGC in the next parliamentary session.
Two men accused of Iran-linked surveillance in London are scheduled to face court on April 24, 2026.
Iranian officials' communication issues with Supreme Leader Khamenei were flagged as obstacles to US peace negotiations as of April 19, 2026.
Iran keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed to leverage better terms from the US in ongoing negotiations.

regime_fall.current_status: The 60-day US-Iran conflict has escalated beyond ceasefire with Trump's shoot-on-sight orders for Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, direct tanker seizures causing 10%+ GDP loss, and IRGC retaliatory vessel seizures. Tehran's leadership fractures under simultaneous military pressure, economic collapse, internal unrest requiring foreign militia deployment, and succession uncertainty as Ghalibaf resigns and Araghchi pursues diplomatic off-ramps in Islamabad while hardliners like Vahidi block negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 24, 2026, Trump ordered US forces to 'shoot and kill' Iranian vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz and enforced naval blockade with direct tanker seizures, while the Treasury froze $344M in Iranian cryptocurrency and sanctioned Chinese refineries, marking full economic-military escalation after ceasefire expiration on April 22.
